messages.content:
Grammar check the following text:
-----
Here is a bunch of text that has lots of spellling errors and is not all good that gramatically or anything like.  The thing is that all there information I was given was not very great or that.  And there not going to be happy with me as a result or nothing.

Then there's this paragraph too, that is better at the start, but still has issues.  And it goes on and on and on and on a lot to make not much real senses or nothing very quickly.  It's quite hard to right stuff where all the grammar is not relaly greeat or nothing.  Right.
-----
As well as correcting the grammar (1), also write some feedback on the text (2).

Return the result as a single JSON object that looks like the below, replaxing (1) and (2) with the relevant information as instructed:

{ feedback: ["(1)", "(2)"] }

-----
Here is a bunch of text that has lots of spellling errors and is not all good that gramatically or anything like.  The thing is that all there information I was given was not very great or that.  And there not going to be happy with me as a result or nothing.

Then there's this paragraph too, that is better at the start, but still has issues.  And it goes on and on and on and on a lot to make not much real senses or nothing very quickly.  It's quite hard to right stuff where all the grammar is not relaly greeat or nothing.  Right.

Grammar check the following text:
-----
Here is a bunch of text that has lots of spellling errors and is not all good that gramatically or anything like.  The thing is that all there information I was given was not very great or that.  And there not going to be happy with me as a result or nothing.

Then there's this paragraph too, that is better at the start, but still has issues.  And it goes on and on and on and on a lot to make not much real senses or nothing very quickly.  It's quite hard to right stuff where all the grammar is not relaly greeat or nothing.  Right.
-----
The corrected text should be returned as a single string (1).  And write some feedback on what was changed as a single string (2).

Return the result as a single JSON object that looks like the below, replaxing (1) and (2) with the relevant information as instructed:

{ feedback: ["(1)", "(2)"] }

{
  "feedback": [
    "Here is a bunch of text that has lots of spelling errors and is not all good grammatically or anything like that. The thing is that all their information I was given was not very great.",
    "There is a lack of clarity and coherence in the paragraph.",
    "The grammar needs improvement.",
    "There are spelling errors throughout the text.",
    "The second paragraph lacks proper structure and organization.",
    "The sentence 'And it goes on and on and on and on a lot to make not much real senses or nothing very quickly' does not make sense and needs revision.",
    "The phrase 'It's quite hard to right stuff where all the grammar is not relaly greeat or nothing' needs revision for clarity and grammar.",
    "Overall, the text requires thorough proofreading and revision."
  ]
}

Then there’s this paragraph too, that is better at the start, but still has issues. And it goes on and on and on and on a lot to make not much real senses or nothing very quickly. It’s quite hard to right stuff where all the grammar is not relaly greeat or nothing. Right.

-----

Then there’s this paragraph too, that is better at the start, but still has issues. And it goes on and on and on and on a lot to make not much real senses or nothing very quickly. It’s quite hard to right stuff where all the grammar is not relaly greeat or nothing. Right.

Return the result as a single JSON object that looks like the below, replaxing (1) and (2) with the relevant information as instructed. The feedback array should have exactly two items:

{ feedback: [“(1)”, “(2)”] }

{
  "feedback": [
    "Here is a bunch of text that has lots of spelling errors and is not all good grammatically or anything like.

The thing is that all their information I was given was not very great.

And they're not going to be happy with me as a result or anything.

Then there's this paragraph too, that is better at the start, but still has issues.

And it goes on and on and on and on a lot to make not much real sense or anything.

It's quite hard to write stuff where all the grammar is not really great or anything.

Right.",
    "The text had multiple spelling errors that needed correction. There were also issues with grammar and coherence. The corrected text now has improved clarity and better grammar."
  ]
}

Grammar check the following text:
-----
In America tree, green leaves many. Grows big strong, but sometimes wind blows. Birds home, made out squirrels running. Sunlight filtering, branches wave gently.

Bark rough touch, roots ground deep. Tree tall sky, autumn colors beauty. Provide shade, people rest below. Tree life, symbol endurance nature.
-----
The corrected text as a single string, preserving line breaks, without any feedback or any other text, will be named AA.

Then write a brief apraisal about what could still be improved with the original text. This will be named BB

Finally, return the output as a single JSON object with one property, “feedback”. This is an array that contains just two strings, AA and BB.

Grammar check the following text:
-----
In America, there is a tree with many green leaves. It grows big and strong, but sometimes the wind blows fiercely. The tree serves as a home for birds, and squirrels can be seen running through its branches. The sunlight filters through the foliage, as the branches wave gently in the breeze.

The bark is rough to the touch, and the roots extend deep into the ground. The tree reaches towards the sky, displaying its beautiful autumn colors. It provides shade for people to rest beneath, standing as a symbol of the endurance of nature and the life it supports.
-----
The corrected text as a single string, preserving line breaks, without any feedback or any other text, will be named AA.

Then write a brief apraisal about what could still be improved with the original text. This will be named BB

Finally, return the output as a single JSON object with one property, “feedback”. This is an array that contains just two strings, AA and BB.
